## [15:07 - 18:08]

And it's just as easy as that to save that and get that live. All right. So now I want to talk about sign up forms. There's tons of form builders out there. There's claveo, there's just, you know, there's Sumo meet, there's tons of different form builders out there. So even though claveo in my personal opinion, doesn't has as good forms as some other third party apps, I still personally prefer to use claveo. And we're switching a number of our clients away from other apps to claveo. And the reason for that is I believe that when someone uses a claveo form, and they have a subscriber sign up, it's better and easier at tying cookies to that profile. So for example, if someone visits your site, and you're using another form builder, that email address will definitely still be passed through to claveo. That's not the question at all. But one thing at least that we've noticed through our personal experience. And again, this isn't something that we've seen documented. This is just based off the 30 clients that we're working with is the fact that when someone visits your site, enters their email until claveo form, it seems like that cookie. And by cookie, I mean, having someone's IP and information tracked to an event that they're doing. So someone goes onto your website, they enter their email into a sign up form, and then they view a product, maybe they add it to their cart, you know, they start check out all of those different types of things or actions that we want to tie to a specific user. And when you're using claveo's native form builder, it just feels like that cookie is there and it follows the user a lot better. Whereas sometimes we've noticed with other form builders, while the email is being passed through, we oftentimes don't get to see what someone's looking at and viewing until their second time back, which is too late. So again, that's personally why we at boundless like to use claveo forms. And again, the more cookies you can get in your customers, the better and the more accurate data you can collect, which will allow you to do a lot more advanced targeting and personalization of your method and offers. And one thing I wanted to point out, so this is a client of ours, they sell custom swimwear goggles. For us, it's really important to know what kind of athlete someone is, whether they're a swimmer or a triathlete. So we're quizzing them in this form. And you know, this form performs pretty well. In claveo, you can actually see under the analytics thing, they have something called forms overview. So you could see over here that 10% of the people that have engaged with this form have actually entered their email. So one out of every 10 people that visits our website ends up entering their email, which is pretty good. I'd say most people are probably collecting, maybe three to 7% of their traffic into email subscribers. So 10, 15, 20 are all incredible numbers. While most of our clients are probably at 10, 13, 15, and some are at 17. So I would strive to be at around 10 plus percent. Again, claveo makes this really easy. And one other thing I wanted to call out is having the different functionalities and features here is really great. So you can kind of adjust the style.

## [18:08 - 21:09]

But more importantly, here's about the behavior. So in this client's example in particular, we are testing different time delays. And in this case, having this pop up in this form load after 12 seconds of someone landing on your site, tend to be the best. We will not show someone this form again, if they've closed it in the past 30 days. So people can only receive this form once a month. This is not going to show to any of our existing claveo profiles. So again, we don't want to show forms to people already on our list. There's no point. And we're also not going to be showing this on certain URLs. So we're not showing this to people when they're further down the funnel on the abandoned cart page or something else. So that's a little bit about forms. And I hope that is helpful. So again, in this particular example, I just showed you the way that we're triggering this pop up is after 12 seconds of someone busy in the site. There's some other ways that you could trigger it as well. So for example, claveo recommends that you trigger this pop up after about three seconds. The reason being is you don't want to show it immediately. Because when someone lands on the page, you want them to be able to explore and figure out a little bit about who you are. Because depending on how they found you, they might have some context or they might not have any context at all. So about three seconds after someone lands on the page is what claveo recommends. So it's not too soon, but it's also not taking forever. Again, in this particular case, we have tested three seconds, six seconds, nine seconds and 12 seconds, and 12 seconds seemed to be the best performing. So that's kind of why we left it here. Another really popular way that you can display a form is upon exit intent. So if someone's leaving your website, you can then trigger the form to send them a pop up that says, hey, before you go, or it could even be the same type of pop up we're looking at here. And the different types of forms that you can create are this, which is a pop up. You also could create something called a fly out, which does exactly that. So typically from the right hand corner, this thing just kind of pops out. It's not obtrusive where the forms like this are a little bit more obtrusive. And then lastly, you could have something like an embedded form. So typically on the bottom of people's websites, they'll have an embedded subscription form that says, enter your email for 10% off that obviously lives there. Doesn't matter how many times people scroll up or down, it's going to always be there. So those are a couple of ways that you could go about triggering forms and kind of a few ways that you can delay them and a few different options of form that we have. All right, so now we'll talk about building an engaged segment. This will be the list that you'll mail most frequently. If you don't send as frequently, if you send only maybe once a month, then you can expand it out. So that way you're not being too restricting.
